name: Queen Mary University of London
summary: Degree-level computer science covering software engineering, algorithms, databases, web development, and applied problem solving.
description: This qualification developed a strong foundation in programming, algorithms, data structures, databases, software engineering, web technologies, and problem-solving. It also built experience in planning, implementing, testing, and presenting technical work through individual and group projects.

name: Exeter College
qualification: A-Levels in Computer Science, Mathematics, English Language
summary: A-Level study combining technical programming, mathematical reasoning, and written communication.
description: This education strengthened computational thinking, programming fundamentals, mathematical reasoning, analytical writing, and communication. It provided preparation for degree-level computer science through both technical and academic work.

name: South Devon College
summary: Computing qualification focused on practical IT, programming basics, and digital systems.
description: This qualification introduced practical computing concepts including digital systems, basic programming, IT workflows, and applied technical problem-solving. It helped establish the practical foundation for later software and computer science study.

name: Romain Rolland Secondary School
summary: General secondary education covering core academic subjects and foundational study skills.
description: This stage built the academic foundations for later technical education, including structured learning, problem-solving habits, communication, and broader subject knowledge.
